purpose: Adds a guard in hci_cmd_sync_queue to return -ENODEV when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing commands from being queued after the device is being unregistered, which would otherwise cause a use‑after‑free.

The commit deletes an unnecessary #include <asm/termios.h> from drivers/tty/n_hdlc.c because the required termios definitions are already provided via linux/tty.h, which pulls linux/termios.h. No functional code changes are made.
The commit adds explanatory text to the perf c2c documentation, describing the newly introduced 'N/A' store metric for Store Reference and Store Refs entries.

The patch fixes incorrect handling of the hugetlb vma lock during VMA splitting and range unmapping. It allocates a fresh vma lock for duplicated VMAs, frees the lock instead of clearing VM_MAYSHARE, and adds proper lock acquisition/release around unmap operations to eliminate races and double‑free scenarios.

The commit updates the device tree binding documentation for the Realtek rt5682s codec by adding descriptions for AVDD-supply and MICVDD-supply properties, marking them as required, and extending the example node with these properties.
The commit adds a missing iounmap() call in the driver’s remove function to correctly release a memory mapping that was allocated during probe, fixing a resource leak.
The commit deletes an unused struct member (work_struct work) from mlx5e_async_ctx in the kTLS driver, cleaning up dead code.
The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned (u8) to signed (s8) to allow a sentinel value of -1 when no key is present, eliminating static analysis warnings.

The commit removes a commented-out CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS line from two RISC-V defconfig files to keep the entries sorted, matching the order produced by savedefconfig. No functional code is altered.

Introduces an inline helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() that returns true when the controller is in CAN‑FD or listen‑only mode, and replaces direct bit‑mask checks with calls to this function in three driver source files, improving readability and maintainability.

The commit modifies the RTC self‑test suite to handle platforms where the RTC device (/dev/rtc) is absent. It removes the unconditional assertion that opening the device succeeds, adds a check for ENOENT, and skips each test when the device is not present, preventing spurious test failures.

The commit removes a dead store by deleting an unnecessary assignment to the variable 'bytes' in snd_echo_midi_output_write. The change does not alter functionality, only cleans up code to silence a clang static analysis warning.

The commit decouples the rcuscale scalability test from the RCU Tasks subsystem. It removes the unconditional selection of TASKS_RCU in the Kconfig, adds conditional compilation around the tasks_ops definitions in rcuscale.c, and adjusts self‑test configuration files to avoid enabling TASKS_RCU when it is not needed. This reduces kernel size and tracing complexity when RCU Tasks are not present.
The commit corrects a duplicated word in a comment within spte.h, fixing a typo without changing any code behavior.

Reverts a recent change that populated the DCN315 clock table using DCFCLK values. The previous change caused the firmware table to be empty when DCFCLK states were disabled, leading to underflow/no‑read errors. This patch restores the original logic, adds a safe lookup function, and guards against all‑zero tables.

The commit updates the devicetree binding documentation for ARM Mali Bifrost GPUs to include the Renesas RZ/V2L (r9a07g054) SoC. It adds a new compatible string to the list and changes the schema to allow both the existing and new strings.

The commit renames the core structure used by the DesignWare PCIe driver from `pcie_port` to `dw_pcie_rp` and updates all related function signatures and references across the driver files for consistency with the existing naming scheme.

The commit corrects a printf-style format string in the AMD XGBE driver: it changes "%#06hx" to "%#06x" to match the promoted integer type of the argument, eliminating a clang -Wformat warning without altering driver functionality.
The commit corrects the branch range check in MIPS jump label code by casting the upper bound constant to long, ensuring a signed comparison and preventing false warnings for negative offsets.

The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU() and replaces calls to SetHwReg8188EU() with direct calls to rtl8188e_set_p2p_ps_offload_cmd(). It also deletes the corresponding enum entry from hal_intf.h, cleaning up an unnecessary abstraction layer in the Realtek 8188EU staging driver.

The commit adds the 'static' qualifier to a const array definition in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c to silence a sparse warning about the symbol not being declared static, improving code correctness and encapsulation.

The commit updates device‑tree binding documentation for MSM display drivers, removing incorrect interrupt‑flags entries from example nodes because the MDSS interrupt provider supplies only a single cell. It also adds a label to the MDSS node.

The commit introduces allocation handling for array controls in the V4L2 control framework, renaming dynamic‑array specific fields to generic array fields and adding logic to allocate memory for array requests. This prepares the kernel to allow drivers to change the size of control arrays.
The commit adds entries for additional PMIC models and SoC identifiers to the qcom socinfo driver by extending static arrays, thereby enabling the driver to recognize newer hardware platforms.

The commit updates the rtl8xxxu driver to correctly map the chip cut value (0‑15) to its corresponding letter (A‑P) when printing chip information, replacing a switch that only handled A‑E and printed "unknown" for higher values.

The commit removes the redundant 'is_enabled' field and its associated functions from the PRCMU clock driver, because the core clock framework already tracks enable state. It deletes the field from the struct, eliminates enable/disable/is_enabled callbacks, and updates the clk_ops tables accordingly.

The commit reorganizes the handling of CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL conditionals in arch/riscv/mm/init.c: it removes scattered #ifdef blocks, moves external declarations and the pt_ops macro definition closer together, and groups related XIP macros, without altering kernel functionality.

The commit updates the device tree for the sm6350 Sony Xperia Lena board, setting the status of GPI DMA0/1 and QUPv3 ID0/1 nodes to "okay". This enables those hardware blocks so that functional I2C nodes can be added later.

The commit updates only comments in drivers/thermal/rzg2l_thermal.c: it replaces an unclear comment with a clearer description, fixes a typo (caliberation → calibration), and reformats block comments to follow the coding style (/* on a separate line). No functional code changes are introduced.

The commit replaces an endless spin‑wait for a hardware semaphore with a conditional check that logs an error and aborts the release if the semaphore cannot be obtained, preventing a hang when the device is surprise‑removed.

The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC codec driver to replace the old master/slave terminology with the newer provider/consumer terminology, renaming variables and switching to the newer DAI format masks and error messages. No functional behavior is changed.
The commit removes a dead macro definition (IS_SWAPFILE) from fs/nfs/file.c because the macro is always defined elsewhere, cleaning up unnecessary code.

Adds support for handling GTP Echo Request messages and sending proper Echo Response packets for both GTPv0 and GTPv1-U, introduces a restart counter field, new netlink attribute, and related routing helpers.

The commit replaces uses of cpumask_next(-1, ...) with cpumask_first(...) where the start index is -1 (i.e., beginning of the mask), adds a missing cpumask_first_zero helper, and implements it in cpumask.h. These changes affect several drivers and core code, improving efficiency and simplifying the API.

The commit removes the driver’s attempt to read the TIM information via the "dtim_assoc" IOVAR after a roam. Firmware no longer supports this IOVAR, causing repeated error messages ("wl dtim_assoc failed (-52)"). The code that parsed TLVs, checked for the TIM IE, and queried the IOVAR is deleted, and the variable initialization is simplified.

Adds a new board configuration 'adl_rt5682' for Intel SOF audio driver to support the alc5682 headset codec without a speaker amplifier, by inserting a new entry in the platform device ID table and a matching ACPI machine entry, connecting the codec to SSP0.

The commit adds a runtime check in io_sendmsg_zc to verify that the socket supports zero‑copy (ZC). If the socket lacks the SOCK_SUPPORT_ZC flag, the function now returns -EOPNOTSUPP, preventing unsupported zerocopy sendmsg operations.

The commit modifies Documentation/sphinx/kerneldoc-preamble.sty to conditionally load the xeCJK LaTeX package only when both the Noto Sans CJK font and the xeCJK.sty file are present, preventing build failures on systems where texlive-xecjk is missing. It adds a boolean flag, file existence checks, and updates notes accordingly.
The commit modifies scripts/checkpatch.pl to add an exception for lines starting with 'case', preventing the checkpatch tool from incorrectly flagging macros that expand to case statements as errors.

The commit rewrites the simpledrm driver to use the generic DRM atomic helper infrastructure instead of the older simple‑KMS helpers. It adds plane, crtc, encoder objects, replaces simple display pipe callbacks with atomic plane/CRTC helper functions, updates includes, and removes the simple‑KMS helper usage. No functional behavior is changed.

The commit introduces a new JPEG decode worker infrastructure for MediaTek MT8195, enabling three hardware decode units to operate in parallel. It adds hardware allocation helpers, a worker function, multithreaded device run handling, new m2m operations, and variant registration for the decoder, along with necessary synchronization primitives.

The commit removes numerous VPRINTK() debug print statements and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper, as these messages are already covered by existing tracepoints. It also deletes the corresponding function prototype from the header. No functional behavior is altered.

The commit adds support for using the extra vertical blanking period (extended vblank) to enter zstate (low-power state) when FreeSync is active. It introduces a new capability flag, debug option, stream update fields, hardware programming functions, and integrates the logic into bandwidth optimization and DML calculations. The changes span multiple driver files, enabling the driver to decide if extended blank is supported, compute optimized timing, and program the hardware accordingly.

The commit updates the function pointer prototype for hqd_destroy in kgd_kfd_interface.h, changing the third parameter type from uint32_t to enum kfd_preempt_type to match actual implementations and eliminate a CFI failure observed when CONFIG_CFI_CLANG is enabled.

The commit corrects a typo in the device tree binding documentation for STM32 pinctrl, changing the property name from 'reset' to the correct 'resets', and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to enforce stricter validation of GPIO node properties.
The commit adds a conditional compiler flag (-Wno-array-bounds) to the ath9k Makefile to suppress a false positive GCC 12 array‑bounds warning in mac.c, without changing any driver functionality.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss PRNG driver, removing an extra pm_runtime_put_noidle call and simplifying error handling.

The commit refactors io_uring fixed file handling by extracting installation and removal helpers into the filetable module, changing their signatures to accept a io_ring_ctx instead of a request, and adding a new removal function used by the close path.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU. It replaces the single "nxp,imx8mq-vpu" node with separate G1 and G2 decoder nodes, each having its own compatible string, reg, interrupt, and clock entries. The compatible property now supports a deprecated old value and two new values. MaxItems for reg, interrupts, and clocks are reduced to 1, and obsolete name properties are removed. Example DT snippets for the new nodes are added.
The commit removes the unused IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and merges its contents directly into the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, simplifying the driver configuration code without changing functionality.
